question: Why must air tanks be drained?
options: To drain all the air out of the tank
explanation: Compressed air usually has some water and some compressor oil in it, which is bad for the air brake system. For example, the water can freeze in cold weather and cause brake failure.

question: The air compressor governor controls:
options: The air pressure in the brake chambers, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
The speed of the air compressor, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
When the brake lights turn on, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
When the air compressor pumps air into the storage tanks
correct_answer: When the air compressor pumps air into the storage tanks
explanation: The governor controls when the air compressor will pump air into the air storage tanks.

question: The air compressor governor should cut out the air compressor at about:
explanation: When air tank pressure rises to the 'cut-out' level (around 125 psi), the governor stops the compressor from pumping air.

question: The driver must be able to see a warning before air pressure in the service air tanks falls below:
explanation: A low air pressure warning signal is required on vehicles with air brakes. It must come on before the air pressure in the tanks falls below 60 psi.

question: If your truck or bus has dual parking control valves, you can use pressure from a separate tank to:
options: Release the spring emergency/parking brakes to move a short distance
Balance the weight of the vehicle, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Stay parked indefinitely, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Apply more pressure for braking, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Release the spring emergency/parking brakes to move a short distance
explanation: Dual parking control valves allow you to use air pressure from a separate tank to release the spring brakes to move the vehicle in an emergency.

question: The brake system that applies and releases the brakes when the driver uses the brake pedal is the:
explanation: The service brake system applies and releases the brakes when you use the brake pedal during normal driving.

question: Your truck or bus has a dual air brake system. If a low air pressure warning comes on for the secondary system, you should:
options: Use the parking brake to stop, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Ignore it if the primary system is working, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Reduce your speed and drive to the nearest garage, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Bring the vehicle to a safe stop and continue only when the system is fixed
correct_answer: Bring the vehicle to a safe stop and continue only when the system is fixed
explanation: If the warning light comes on, either the front or rear brakes have failed. It is not safe to continue driving.

question: How should you check that your service brakes are working properly?
options: Listen for air leaks while driving, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures, as long as all safety systems have been tested and verified
Park on a hill and see if the vehicle rolls, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles, unless directed otherwise by law enforcement or a flag person
Wait for normal air pressure, release the parking brake, move forward slowly, and apply the brakes firmly using the brake pedal
Pump the brake pedal three times and hold it, after confirming all safety checks have been completed, as long as all safety systems have been tested and verified
correct_answer: Wait for normal air pressure, release the parking brake, move forward slowly, and apply the brakes firmly using the brake pedal
explanation: This test confirms the brakes are stopping the vehicle and checks if the vehicle pulls to one side, which could indicate a brake problem.

question: During normal driving, spring brakes are usually held back by:
explanation: Powerful springs are held back by air pressure. If the air pressure is removed, the springs put on the brakes.

question: A straight truck or bus air brake system should not leak at a rate of more than ___ psi per minute with the engine off and the brakes released.
explanation: For a single vehicle (straight truck or bus), the static leakage rate (brakes released) should be less than 2 psi per minute.

question: The safety valve is set to automatically reduce pressure at:
explanation: A safety relief valve protects the tank and the rest of the system from too much pressure. It is usually set to open at 150 psi.

question: Excessive use of the service brakes results in overheating, which can lead to:
explanation: Excessive heat causes chemical changes in the brake lining and expansion of the brake drums, leading to brake fade (loss of stopping power).

question: If you are away from your vehicle only a short time, you do not need to use the parking brake.
explanation: Whenever you park, use the parking brakes, except when the brakes are very hot or wet in freezing temperatures.

question: How do you check the slack adjusters?
options: Park on level ground, turn off the parking brakes, and pull hard on each slack adjuster
Measure the distance from the ground, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Apply the service brakes and measure the push rod travel, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Look at them to see if they are broken, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Park on level ground, turn off the parking brakes, and pull hard on each slack adjuster
explanation: If a slack adjuster moves more than about one inch where the push rod attaches to it, it probably needs adjustment.

question: The air loss rate for a combination vehicle (with air brakes) with the engine off and the brakes applied should not be more than:
explanation: For a combination vehicle, the applied leakage rate (brakes held down) must be less than 4 psi per minute.

question: If the air pressure is not built up fast enough within the correct time:
options: The air pressure may drop too low during driving
The alcohol evaporator is full, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
You should drive slower, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
The governor is set too high, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: The air pressure may drop too low during driving
explanation: If air pressure does not build up fast enough, the pressure may drop too low during driving, requiring an emergency stop.

question: The S-cam:
options: Is used only in hydraulic brakes, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Forces the brake shoes against the inside of the brake drum
Controls the air flow to the brake chamber, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Is a type of emergency brake, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Forces the brake shoes against the inside of the brake drum
explanation: When you push the brake pedal, air is let into the brake chamber. The S-cam forces the brake shoes away from one another and presses them against the inside of the brake drum.

question: Under normal conditions, in order to engage the parking brakes the driver must:
options: Pull the parking brake control knob out
Push down the brake pedal, provided the load is properly secured and within legal limits
Turn off the engine, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Let the air out of the air brake system
correct_answer: Pull the parking brake control knob out
explanation: The parking brake control is a diamond-shaped, yellow, push-pull control knob. You pull the knob out to put the parking brakes on.

question: Tractor and straight truck spring brakes will come fully on when air pressure drops to a range of:
explanation: If air pressure drops to a range of 20 to 45 psi, the spring brakes will come on automatically.

question: When driving a vehicle with a dual air system, you should allow time for the air compressor to build up a minimum of ____ psi pressure in both the primary and secondary systems.
explanation: Before driving a vehicle with a dual air system, allow time for the air compressor to build up a minimum of 100 psi pressure in both systems.

question: What is the function of the alcohol evaporator?
options: To lubricate the compressor, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
To clean the air, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
To cool the engine, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
To put alcohol into the air system to reduce the risk of ice
correct_answer: To put alcohol into the air system to reduce the risk of ice
explanation: The alcohol evaporator puts alcohol vapor into the air system to prevent ice from forming in cold weather.

question: The brake pedal in an air brake system:
options: Is only used for parking, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Controls the engine speed, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Controls the air pressure applied to the brakes
Is connected directly to the brake shoes
correct_answer: Controls the air pressure applied to the brakes
explanation: Pushing the pedal harder applies more air pressure. Letting up on the pedal releases the air pressure.

question: Foundation brakes are used at:
explanation: Foundation brakes are the mechanical parts at each wheel that stop the vehicle.

question: The most common type of foundation brake is the:
explanation: S-cam drum brakes are the most common type found on heavy vehicles.

question: Which of these is NOT a part of a drum brake system?
explanation: The safety valve is part of the air tank system, not the foundation brake at the wheel.

question: What happens when you push the brake pedal?
options: Air pressure enters the brake chamber
Air pressure leaves the brake chamber
The spring brakes apply, when hauling hazardous materials over a designated route
The compressor stops, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Air pressure enters the brake chamber
explanation: Pushing the pedal lets air into each brake chamber, which pushes the push rod out.

question: What does the supply pressure gauge tell you?
options: The tire pressure, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
How much pressure you are applying to the brakes
How much pressure is in the air tanks
The engine temperature, when hauling hazardous materials over a designated route
correct_answer: How much pressure is in the air tanks
explanation: These gauges tell you how much air pressure is available in the air tanks.

question: What does the application pressure gauge tell you?
options: How much air is in the tanks, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
When to shift gears, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
How much air pressure you are applying to the brakes
How fast you are going, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: How much air pressure you are applying to the brakes
explanation: This gauge shows how much air pressure you are applying to the brakes. (Not on all vehicles).

question: A low air pressure warning signal is required on vehicles with air brakes. This signal must come on before the pressure drops below:
explanation: The warning must activate before pressure drops below 60 psi (or one-half the compressor governor cutout pressure on older vehicles).

question: The stop light switch:
options: Turns on the headlights, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Turns on the brake lights when you put on the air brakes
Turns off the engine, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Warns of low air pressure, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Turns on the brake lights when you put on the air brakes
explanation: The air brake system does this using an electric switch that works by air pressure.

question: Front brake limiting valves were used to:
options: Save air, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Cool the brakes, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Reduce the chance of the front wheels skidding on slippery surfaces
Increase braking power, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Reduce the chance of the front wheels skidding on slippery surfaces
explanation: They reduced air to the front brakes by 50%. They are not found on newer vehicles.

question: On newer vehicles with automatic front wheel limiting valves, the control is:
options: A foot pedal, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
A switch on the dash, as specified in the state commercial driver licensing manual
A lever, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
explanation: These valves reduce air to the front brakes automatically during hard braking on slippery surfaces.

question: Spring brakes are:
options: Hydraulic, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Only for parking, only after completing a thorough pre-trip safety inspection
explanation: Spring brakes are mechanical brakes held in the released position by air pressure.

question: If the air pressure is removed, the spring brakes will:
explanation: Without air pressure to hold them back, the springs expand and apply the brakes.

question: Tractor and straight truck spring brakes will come fully on when air pressure drops to a range of: (Safety)
explanation: Automatic application happens when pressure drops to 20-45 psi.

question: The parking brake control on newer vehicles is usually a:
options: Square green button, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Red, octagonal knob, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Round blue knob, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
explanation: This is the standard control for parking brakes.

question: You should never push the brake pedal down when the spring brakes are on. Why?
options: It will waste fuel, as specified in the state commercial driver licensing manual
explanation: The combined force of the springs and the air pressure could damage the brakes.

question: Vehicles with air brakes must have:
explanation: To monitor the air system, an air pressure gauge is required.

question: A dual air brake system has two separate air brake systems, which use a single set of brake controls. Each system has its own:
options: Engine, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Brake pedal, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Steering wheel, when operating a vehicle with a GVWR over 26,001 pounds
explanation: They share controls but have separate plumbing and tanks.

question: In a dual air system, the primary system usually operates the:
explanation: Typically, the primary system operates the rear brakes, and the secondary operates the front.

question: Before driving a vehicle with a dual air system, allow time for the air compressor to build up a minimum of ____ psi pressure in both the primary and secondary systems.
explanation: Wait for at least 100 psi in both systems before driving.

question: During the final step of the pre-trip inspection, you should test the:
options: Radio, as specified in the state commercial driver licensing manual
explanation: Move forward slowly and apply the brakes to check for stopping power and pull.

question: To check the free play of manual slack adjusters on S-cam brakes, you should park on level ground and:
options: Chock the wheels and turn off the parking brakes
Start the engine, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Drain the air tanks, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Apply the parking brakes, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Chock the wheels and turn off the parking brakes
explanation: You must release the parking brakes to check slack, so chocking wheels is essential for safety.

question: If a slack adjuster moves more than about ____ inch where the push rod attaches to it, it probably needs adjustment.
explanation: More than 1 inch of play usually indicates a need for adjustment.

question: The air compressor drive belt should be checked for tightness and condition. It is tight if it deflects less than ____ inch when pushed firmly.
explanation: Check belts for excessive wear and proper tension (less than 3/4 inch deflection).

question: To test the low pressure warning signal, you should:
options: Shut the engine off and pump the brake pedal
Accelerate quickly to build up system pressure before stopping
Drain the tanks, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Disconnect the battery, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Shut the engine off and pump the brake pedal
explanation: With the key on and engine off, pump the brakes to lower pressure until the warning activates.

question: To check that the spring brakes come on automatically, you should:
options: Pull the knob out, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Drive the vehicle away immediately after uncoupling the trailer
Continue pumping the brakes until the parking brake knob pops out
Turn off the key, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Continue pumping the brakes until the parking brake knob pops out
explanation: Pump the brakes to lower pressure to 20-45 psi, causing the spring brakes to activate.

question: When testing the air leakage rate for a single vehicle, the pressure should not drop more than ____ psi in one minute with the brakes released.
explanation: Static leakage rate for a single vehicle is 2 psi/minute.

question: When testing the air leakage rate for a single vehicle, the pressure should not drop more than ____ psi in one minute with the brakes applied.
explanation: Applied leakage rate for a single vehicle is 3 psi/minute.

question: When testing the air leakage rate for a combination vehicle, the pressure should not drop more than ____ psi in one minute with the brakes released.
explanation: Static leakage rate for a combination vehicle is 3 psi/minute.

question: When testing the air leakage rate for a combination vehicle, the pressure should not drop more than ____ psi in one minute with the brakes applied.
explanation: Applied leakage rate for a combination vehicle is 4 psi/minute.

question: To check the air compressor governor cut-in and cut-out pressures, you should:
options: Drive on the highway, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Feel the compressor, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Listen for the compressor, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Run the engine at a fast idle and watch the air gauge
correct_answer: Run the engine at a fast idle and watch the air gauge
explanation: Watch for the pressure to stop rising (cut-out) and start rising (cut-in).

question: Normal stopping distance with air brakes is longer than with hydraulic brakes because of:
explanation: It takes time for the air to flow through the lines to the brakes.

question: Brake lag adds about ____ feet to your stopping distance at 55 mph.
explanation: At 55 mph, brake lag adds about 32 feet.

question: Excessive heat in the brakes can cause:
explanation: Heat causes chemical changes and drum expansion, reducing braking power.

question: The proper braking technique for going down long, steep grades is called:
explanation: Apply brakes to slow down 5 mph below safe speed, release, and repeat.

question: If your low air pressure warning comes on while driving, you should:
options: Speed up, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Ignore it, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Stop and safely park your vehicle as soon as possible
Drive to the next exit, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Stop and safely park your vehicle as soon as possible
explanation: You may lose braking ability soon, so stop immediately.

question: Parking brakes should be used:
options: Never, only when driving under a special state or federal permit
explanation: Always use parking brakes when parked, with few exceptions.

question: You should NOT use the parking brakes if:
options: You are tired, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
You are on a hill, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
The brakes are very hot or wet in freezing temperatures
It is raining, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: The brakes are very hot or wet in freezing temperatures
explanation: Hot brakes can be damaged by the pressure; wet brakes can freeze.

question: If you must park without using the parking brakes because they are wet and freezing, you should:
explanation: Chock the wheels to prevent movement until the brakes dry out.

question: An air tank is equipped with a drain valve to remove:
options: Dirt, provided all air pressure gauges read within normal operating range
Fuel, as specified in the state commercial driver licensing manual
Air, as specified in the state commercial driver licensing manual
explanation: Water and oil accumulate in the tank and must be drained.

question: The S-cam forces the brake shoes against the inside of the brake drum.
explanation: The S-cam rotates and pushes the shoes out.

question: Wedge brakes require manual adjustment.
explanation: Wedge brakes usually have automatic adjusters.

question: Disc brakes use a ____ instead of a cam.
explanation: Air pressure acts on a brake chamber and slack adjuster, turning a power screw.

question: All air brake equipped vehicles have:
options: A speedometer, once the vehicle has been inspected and cleared for operation
A tachometer, once the vehicle has been inspected and cleared for operation
A radio, after confirming all safety checks have been completed

question: The application pressure gauge shows how much air pressure you are:
options: Losing, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Generating, unless directed otherwise by law enforcement or a flag person
Storing, when hauling hazardous materials over a designated route
explanation: Shows the pressure being delivered to the brake chambers.

question: If the air compressor develops a leak, what keeps the air in the tanks?
options: The governor, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
explanation: The check valve prevents air from flowing back out of the tank.

question: In a dual air system, the air pressure should be at least ____ psi in each system before driving.
explanation: 100 psi is the minimum safe operating pressure.

question: How often should you drain automatic air tanks?
options: It is still good practice to check them daily
Monthly, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Weekly, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Never, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: It is still good practice to check them daily
explanation: Even automatic drains should be checked to ensure they are working.

question: The brake drums or discs must not have cracks longer than ____ the width of the friction area.
explanation: Cracks longer than half the width are dangerous.

question: Brake linings (friction material) must not be loose or soaked with:
options: Dust, provided all air pressure gauges read within normal operating range
Air, as specified in the state commercial driver licensing manual
explanation: Oil or grease reduces friction and braking power.

question: Brake linings should not be dangerously thin. True or False?
explanation: Thin linings can fail or damage the drums.

question: Mechanical slack adjusters should be adjusted:
explanation: Manual slack adjusters require manual adjustment.

question: Automatic slack adjusters adjust themselves during:
options: Acceleration, unless directed otherwise by law enforcement or a flag person
Idling, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Parking, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
explanation: They adjust when the brakes are applied.

question: The air compressor is connected to the engine through:
options: Wires, as specified in the state commercial driver licensing manual
Chain, after verifying all brake components are properly adjusted
Hoses, only when driving under a special state or federal permit

question: The air compressor may be air cooled or cooled by the:
options: Wind, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Fuel, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Oil, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
explanation: It can share the engine's cooling system.

question: If the compressor has its own oil supply, you should check the oil level:
The safety valve is usually located on the:
explanation: It protects the tank from over-pressure.

question: The brake pedal is also known as the:
options: Hand valve, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Check valve, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Relay valve, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
explanation: Common names for the brake pedal assembly.

question: Pushing the brake pedal harder applies ____ air pressure.
explanation: Pressure is proportional to pedal effort.

question: Letting up on the brake pedal ____ the air pressure.
explanation: Releasing the pedal exhausts the air.

question: Pressing and releasing the brake pedal unnecessarily can:
options: Cool the brakes, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Let air out faster than the compressor can replace it
Increase pressure, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Save fuel, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Let air out faster than the compressor can replace it
explanation: This can deplete the air supply.

question: The most common foundation brake found on heavy vehicles is the:
Brake drums are located:
options: On the driveshaft, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
In the cab, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
On each end of the vehicle's axles
In the engine, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: On each end of the vehicle's axles
explanation: They are part of the wheel assembly.

question: The brake shoes and linings are pushed against the:
options: Tire, unless the vehicle is equipped with an automatic safety override
Rim, unless the vehicle is equipped with an automatic safety override
explanation: Friction against the inside of the drum creates stopping force.

question: S-cam brakes are applied by:
options: Turning a key, when operating a vehicle with a GVWR over 26,001 pounds
explanation: The pedal initiates the air flow.

question: When the brake pedal is released, the ____ pulls the brake shoes away from the drum.
Wedge brakes push the shoes out using a:
explanation: The push rod pushes a wedge directly between the shoes.

question: Disc brakes use ____ instead of brake shoes.
The supply pressure gauge is sometimes called the:
options: Speedometer, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Primary and secondary air pressure gauge
Fuel gauge, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Odometer, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Primary and secondary air pressure gauge

question: If the air pressure falls below ____ psi, the low air pressure warning must come on.
The low air pressure warning is usually a:
A buzzer may also come on when air pressure is low.
The wig wag is a type of:
options: Light, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Switch, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
The primary brake actuator connected to the trailer service brake circuit
explanation: A mechanical arm that drops into view when pressure is low.

question: The wig wag drops into view when pressure drops below:
The stop light switch is operated by:
explanation: Air pressure triggers the electric switch.

question: Spring brakes are used for:
options: Service brakes, once the vehicle has been inspected and cleared for operation
A secondary steering mechanism used on multi-axle trailer configurations
Acceleration, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
explanation: They provide braking when air is lost or for parking.

question: Spring brakes are not used on:
options: Buses, as specified in the state commercial driver licensing manual
Trucks, following established DOT pre-trip inspection requirements
explanation: Older vehicles might not have them.

question: When the parking brakes are on, the spring brakes are:
explanation: Parking brakes use the spring mechanism.

question: The parking brake control knob is:
You pull the parking brake knob ____ to apply the brakes.
explanation: Pull to apply, push to release.

question: You push the parking brake knob ____ to release the brakes.
On older vehicles, the parking brake may be controlled by a:
Use the parking brakes whenever you park.
Never push the brake pedal down when the spring brakes are on.
Modulating control valves allow you to:
options: Control the radio, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Control the lights, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Control the engine, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
explanation: Used to stop the vehicle in an emergency if service brakes fail.

question: Dual parking control valves allow you to:
options: Park faster, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Apply the brakes harder, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Drive faster, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Release the spring brakes using air from a separate tank
correct_answer: Release the spring brakes using air from a separate tank
explanation: Allows moving the vehicle a short distance.

question: ABS stands for:
explanation: Computerized system to prevent wheel lockup.

question: ABS is an addition to your normal brakes.
explanation: It does not replace the normal braking function.

question: ABS decreases or increases brake pressure to:
options: Go faster, when hauling hazardous materials over a designated route
Stop faster, once the vehicle has been inspected and cleared for operation

question: ABS activates only when:
options: You turn, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Wheels are about to lock up
You brake hard, when operating a vehicle with a GVWR over 26,001 pounds
You drive fast, according to the federal motor carrier safety regulations
correct_answer: Wheels are about to lock up

question: ABS does not:
options: Prevent skids, once the vehicle has been inspected and cleared for operation
Work on ice, as long as all safety systems have been tested and verified
Help steering, when operating a vehicle with a GVWR over 26,001 pounds
explanation: It may not shorten distance, but it allows steering.

question: Vehicles with ABS have ____ lamps to tell you if something is wrong.
options: Green go, only when driving under a special state or federal permit
Blue info, provided the load is properly secured and within legal limits
Red stop, provided the load is properly secured and within legal limits
explanation: Yellow ABS light indicates a fault.

question: Trailers with ABS have a yellow light on the:
explanation: Located on the left side, either front or rear corner.

question: If the ABS light stays on, the ABS is:
If ABS is not working, you still have normal brake functions.
explanation: The underlying air brakes still work.

question: A dual air system consists of:
options: Two engines, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Two compressors, according to the federal motor carrier safety regulations
Two drivers, after confirming all safety checks have been completed

question: The first system is called the:
The other system is called the:
Before driving, you should check the air compressor drive belt.
When checking the air compressor oil level, the ground must be:
explanation: Accurate oil readings require the vehicle to be on level ground.

question: A loose air compressor drive belt can cause:
options: The lights to dim, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
The engine to overheat, only when driving under a special state or federal permit
The brakes to lock, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
The compressor to not pump enough air
correct_answer: The compressor to not pump enough air

question: If the air compressor belt is broken:
options: It will work fine, according to the federal motor carrier safety regulations
It will make noise, provided all air pressure gauges read within normal operating range
It will work better, after verifying all brake components are properly adjusted

question: Manual slack adjusters are found on:
options: Cars, provided all air pressure gauges read within normal operating range

question: To check slack adjusters, you must:
options: Rest in the sleeper berth until the situation resolves itself
Drive the vehicle at highway speed without applying brakes
explanation: The push rod must be free to move.

question: When pulling on a slack adjuster, it should not move more than:
explanation: About 1 inch is the limit for safe adjustment.

question: If a slack adjuster is out of adjustment:
options: It will save fuel, unless the vehicle is equipped with an automatic safety override
explanation: The push rod may bottom out, losing force.

question: Brake drums must not have:
options: Heat, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Rust, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Dust, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Cracks longer than 1/2 the width of the friction area
correct_answer: Cracks longer than 1/2 the width of the friction area
explanation: Large cracks can cause the drum to shatter.

question: Brake linings must not be less than ____ inch thick.
The air leakage rate test is done with the engine:
explanation: Engine must be off to detect leaks without the compressor refilling.

question: Before testing air leakage, you should:
options: Build air pressure to governor cut-out
Disconnect the battery, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Open the door, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Drain the tanks, after confirming all safety checks have been completed
correct_answer: Build air pressure to governor cut-out

question: The low pressure warning signal test is done with the key:
explanation: Electrical power is needed for the light/buzzer.

question: When testing the spring brakes, the parking brake knob should pop out when pressure drops to:
To test the service brakes, you should move forward at about:
explanation: Slow speed is safe for testing.

question: If the vehicle pulls to one side when testing service brakes, it may mean:
options: Bad radio, following established DOT pre-trip inspection requirements
Open window, as long as all safety systems have been tested and verified
Low fuel, after confirming all safety checks have been completed

question: Controlled braking is used to:
options: Save tires, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Stop slowly, as required by FMCSA regulations for commercial vehicles
Turn fast, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
Keep the vehicle in a straight line while braking
correct_answer: Keep the vehicle in a straight line while braking
explanation: Prevents wheel lockup to maintain steering.

question: Stab braking involves:
options: Using the hand brake, according to standard pre-trip inspection procedures
Applying brakes all the way, releasing when wheels lock, repeating
Light braking, only if the vehicle is equipped with the proper safety equipment
